description: Evergreen topic bank for the Visage Aesthetics auto-blog generator. The script picks one topic per run that (a) hasn't been covered yet, (b) has high search intent, and (c) ideally connects to a recent news angle from web search.

evergreenTopics.title: Lip filler aftercare: a complete guide
targetKeywords: what to do after lip filler
evergreenTopics.angle: Calm walkthrough of the first 48 hours, the 2-week settle, what to avoid, when to worry. Bernadette's checklist.

evergreenTopics.title: How long does Botox actually last?
evergreenTopics.angle: 3-4 month answer, plus the variables (dose, area, metabolism, frequency of use) that move the timeline.

evergreenTopics.title: Five mistakes first-time Botox clients make
evergreenTopics.angle: Practical: too much, too soon, wrong clinic, no consultation, no review. Honest.

evergreenTopics.title: Profhilo: a no-nonsense treatment guide
evergreenTopics.angle: What it is, what it isn't, who it works on, who it doesn't. Why a course of two.

evergreenTopics.title: Anti-wrinkle aftercare: the first 24 hours
evergreenTopics.angle: Hour-by-hour. Don't lie down, don't massage, don't exercise hard. Why each rule.

evergreenTopics.title: How to find a natural-looking aesthetics clinic in Essex
evergreenTopics.angle: What to ask in a consultation, what red flags to watch for, why nurse-led matters.

evergreenTopics.title: UK aesthetics regulation in 2026: what's actually changing
evergreenTopics.angle: Latest legislative state of play, what it means for clients, why it matters who you see.

evergreenTopics.title: AQUALYX fat dissolving: what to expect
evergreenTopics.angle: Mechanism, candidate selection, course planning, downtime, realistic results.

evergreenTopics.title: Tear trough filler: when it works and when it really doesn't
evergreenTopics.angle: The hardest area in aesthetics. Why most clients are not candidates, and what to do instead.

evergreenTopics.title: Preventative Botox in your late twenties: yes or no
evergreenTopics.angle: An honest answer for the under-30 demographic. When it makes sense, when it doesn't, what to start with.

evergreenTopics.title: Aesthetic treatment for men: what's different
evergreenTopics.angle: Different anatomy, different dosing, different goals. Subtle is even more important.

evergreenTopics.title: Ten questions to ask at any aesthetics consultation
evergreenTopics.angle: Question 1: NMC pin? Question 2: indemnity insurer? Question 3: reversal protocols on site?

evergreenTopics.title: Micro-needling: when you'll see results, and what to expect
evergreenTopics.angle: Collagen takes time. 4-6 weeks per session, courses of 3, real-life expectations.

evergreenTopics.title: Botox for excessive sweating: a medical use of an aesthetic treatment
evergreenTopics.angle: Mechanism (acetylcholine block), longevity (6+ months), why this works medically.

evergreenTopics.title: HarmonyCa vs traditional dermal filler: when to choose which
evergreenTopics.angle: Hybrid HA + CaHA, biostimulation, who's the right candidate.

evergreenTopics.title: Vitamin B12 injections: when they actually work
evergreenTopics.angle: The science of injectable vs oral, who genuinely benefits, who's wasting money.

newsAngles: MHRA / regulatory updates affecting aesthetic practice
Save Face / JCCP / BACN industry announcements
New product launches in UK aesthetic injectables
Reported complications or industry incidents prompting consumer education
Government licensing scheme rollout for non-surgical cosmetic procedures
Notable celebrity / public conversation shifting consumer awareness
Seasonal trends (wedding season, summer skin prep, winter recovery, NYE)
voiceRules: First-person from Bernadette where appropriate; British English spelling
Calm, considered, never breathy or salesy. No exclamation marks except in quotes.
Concrete numbers and timeframes over vague reassurance
Acknowledge industry weaknesses honestly (unregulated, over-treatment, etc.)
Internal links to /treatments/[relevant] and /about/qualifications where natural
End with a free-consultation CTA, never an aggressive book-now
